D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

Subpixel Layouts

stripe, PenTile diamond, BGR — the arrangement of the tiny lamps changes how sharp text and colour can be

Not every panel lays its subpixels out the same way. The classic RGB stripe gives one red, green, and blue per pixel. PenTile diamond (common on OLED phones) shares green-rich subpixels between pixels, so it has fewer reds and blues and leans on the eye's green sensitivity. BGR and RGBW are other rearrangements.

Switch the layout and see a magnified white field. Stripe is even thirds; PenTile shows its sparse, offset red/blue dots among denser greens. The layout decides how subpixel anti-aliasing and apparent sharpness behave.
